title: Delicate Venezuelan Turkey Burrito
description: A floral, crumbly, energizing low‑calorie burrito that blends seasoned turkey mince with a bright yogurt‑lime sauce and fresh cilantro.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and minced garlic and sauté until the onion becomes translucent.
Add the turkey mince to the skillet, breaking it up with a spoon, and cook until browned and the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C).
Stir in the ground cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper, and cook for an additional minute to toast the spices.
Fold in the chopped cilantro, if using, and cook for 30 seconds before removing from heat.
Warm the whole wheat tortillas in a dry skillet for about 15 seconds each side until soft and pliable.
Spread a thin layer of Greek yogurt on each tortilla and drizzle with lime juice.
Divide the turkey mixture between the tortillas, crumble queso fresco over the filling, and roll tightly to form burritos.
Serve immediately, optionally accompanied by a side of fresh salsa or a light lettuce salad.
